rationale: Review 1's fixes for the redundant `change` (comul_includeRight, lines 170-174, replaceable by the rfl includeRight_apply simp at 137) and `ext+induction+simp` patterns (map_id at 98 and map_comp at 120, which silently discharge non-trivial BialgHom coercions and map equations) are material for proof-quality and directly improve robustness/clarity when implemented; Review 2's simpa-to-simp-only change targets a real but narrower fragility in antipode_comp without addressing the same breadth of hidden automation. Both correctly flag the change, but acting on Review 1 yields the better PR overall.
